title: [Firtool] Conversion of reduced Rocket HW IR to Verilog doesn't terminate or segfaults
body:
Stumbled across another weird issue through circt-reduce - [this file](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21347496/breaking.txt), for me, will fail to terminate, or segfault if I run it with `--debug`, with the following command: `firtool <file> --format=mlir --verilog`.

I found that if I pipe the debug-mode output into a file it won't segfault, but it still doesn't terminate and the debug output seems to be several output streams mangled together, e.g.:

```
eai3% e1%io_in_c_bits_sizet :   in sv.if : }ExportVerilog.cpp:0:0} -> , e sv.if %//===-------------------------------------------===//
ai      rs1arg1413, %r) -> _i_4% auto_icache_master_out_a_bits_address (

success%r : % {c0_i30Processing operation : 'sosv.assertts_, , ifalse_(a, 8%io_enq_bits_size : %i    #16queue_arw_deq_q.io_deq_bits_qos tpattern applied successfullyfalse%
false, hw.constant%'(s_ e2rio_in_6_ready%4,tisin =  : comb.muxifalse3sv.if,  %0x0000784e942a1ed3_
       { 
c0_i320x62fcb8354020ei%9t: arg1414,  o1s i 32 ) -> %queue_arw_deq_q.io_deq_bits_echo_tl_state_size 9// *** IR Dump After Pattern Application ***
sv.assert{       , ) {
rmfalse"_i, i1immediate_ c, e%io_in_c_bits_source4bin, {ifalse, __pthread_once_slow0
%      false%sv.assert  te,_
a%4, labellir : t , in
1 % "sv.assert,false _m     s1192, , %io_in_7_ready i3i_2%in       {queue_arw_deq_q.io_deq_bits_echo_tl_state_source./nptl/pthread_once.c:118:7 

 hw.module, %%false__immediate}siarg1416: "n, 8, 0auto_in_d_ready %auto_icache_master_out_d_validsv.assert
, %#17false 

    immediate c0_i2,ab e
2, iatiin" %io_enq_bits_source, :  i :        %,0x000062fcb1a6cfde}
 private,   @silabel    r, %1s.s3%io_in_c_bits_address
%2i, %sv.assertqueue_arw_deq_q.io_deq_bits_wen  
label   %immediatefalseTLMonitor_6st tsv.if_iarg1417, ae,  :     51,, ininfalse  = immediatestd::__future_base::_Deferred_state<std::thread::_Invoker<std::tuple<std::function<void ()>>>, void>::_M_complete_async()      * Pattern " , label%e(s" 619, io_out_validurii}32
   %io_enq_bits_sink, : %hw.instancelabelExportVerilog.cpp:0:0sv.if : 'a falserin_a%
"%: tt4,     in%%auto_icache_master_out_d_bits_opcode ifalse  
 scomb.extract, "t dsfalse} 
arg1418io_, sv.if 8 : immediatei 2,""#18%s -> (%a_%clockas{    , 
%1__i %io_in_c_bits_corrupt% 3label, ,  qa falsee )' {
sfalse6 : te
}

arg1419, ta2 : false: ininimmediateus0x000062fcb1a6c667r{Trying to match "s, 0!"seqar      t
,     } -> io_out_bits_writels, i  " a  es et
"
e%."
sv.assert_%sv.ifsuccess: _si1{, 
i%auto_icache_master_out_d_bits_sizes : %io_enq_bits_deniedlabelustd::_Function_handler<void (), std::shared_future<void> llvm::ThreadPoolInterface::asyncImpl<void>(std::function<void ()>, llvm::ThreadPoolTaskGroup*)::'lambda'()>::_M_invoke(std::_Any_data const&)r_      "rc0_i30tclock,      _arg1400  : , iie32in       3si :  e t_sv.assert" result , _, "}%a%at least one pattern matched%1nr, sv.assert%io_in_d_ready
e4i"_ExportVerilog.cpp:0:0_
a 0%_ini
falsesfalse
arg1401, _ti  : %  , rin1aa_#19s%
```

so I wasn't sure how to debug this one (though I don't know if the garbage output is due to some parallelism I can disable?).
